title: Acaulimalva weberbaueri
intro: Acaulimalva weberbaueri is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae), native to Peru. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Acaulimalva weberbaueri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Acaulimalva weberbaueri is classified within the family Malvaceae. This family includes over 200 genera and 2,300 species of flowering plants commonly known as mallows, hibiscus, and okra. Acaulimalva weberbaueri is further classified within the tribe Malveae. It is a genus that is primarily endemic to South America. More specifically, it is part of the Malvaceae family within the order Malvales.
